name: node-promise-inflight
title: node-promise-inflight
summary: one promise for multiple requests in flight to avoid async duplication
description:
one promise for multiple requests in flight to avoid async duplication
Multiple requests called with the same result (only one underlying promise)
Based on the callback based function 'inflight'.
.
Usage: return inflight(key, () => {/*fetch url*/ return Promise.delay(100)}
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-promise-inflight because one promise for multiple requests in flight to avoid async duplication.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-promise-inflight as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-promise-inflight with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-promise-inflight --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
